name: Cause-Effect — Publication Bias Root Causes (Fishbone with 5 branches)
input: Create a fishbone diagram analyzing root causes of publication bias in medical research. Effect: Publication Bias. Categories: Researchers (career pressure, positive result preference), Journals (preference for novel findings, rejection of null results), Funding (industry sponsorship, grant renewal pressure), Methodology (underpowered studies, flexible analysis), Peer Review (reviewer bias toward significance, lack of blinding).
qualityCriteria: Items present (Researchers, Journals, Funding, Methodology, Peer Review)
Description for each item is present
No placeholder or lorem ipsum content
Cause and effect branches for each category
napkinBenchmark: Fishbone with central spine pointing to effect, 5 diagonal branches each with 2 sub-causes.

data.title: Root Causes of Publication Bias

items.label: Researchers
items.description: Career pressure to publish positive results; inherent preference for confirming hypotheses

items.label: Journals
items.description: Editorial preference for novel significant findings; systematic rejection of null or negative results

items.label: Funding
items.description: Industry sponsorship bias toward favorable outcomes; grant renewal pressure for positive data

items.label: Methodology
items.description: Underpowered studies with small sample sizes; flexible analysis strategies (p-hacking)

items.label: Peer Review
items.description: Reviewer bias favoring statistically significant results; lack of outcome blinding in review

data.caption: Ishikawa analysis of factors contributing to publication bias in medical literature
